# Integration test for MCP tools: mcpspy, mcp-shadow, mcpcat, mcpdiff, mcp-sort, and mcp-replay
# This test demonstrates the comprehensive workflow of using these tools together

-- test1.mcp --
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1} [2024-01-01 10:00:00.000]
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}} [2024-01-01 10:00:01.000]
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":2} [2024-01-01 10:00:02.000]
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"message":"pong"}} [2024-01-01 10:00:03.000]

-- test2.mcp --
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1} # 1000.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}} # 1001.000
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":2} # 1002.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"message":"pong"}} # 1003.000

-- shadow.mcp --
# mcptrace:v1 compare=true
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1} # 1000.000 spanid=aaa1
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}} # 1001.000 spanid=aaa2
mcp-send-shadow {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"tools":true}}} # 1001.100 spanid=bbb2 linksto=aaa2

-- unsorted.mcp --
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test","id":1} [2024-01-01 10:00:00.500]
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"ok":true}} [2024-01-01 10:00:00.100]
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test2","id":2} [2024-01-01 10:00:00.300]

-- input.txt --
test data

-- test_input.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test","id":1}

-- primary_server.sh --
#!/bin/bash
while IFS= read -r line; do
  if echo "$line" | grep -q "initialize"; then
    echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}}'
  fi
done

-- shadow_server.sh --
#!/bin/bash
while IFS= read -r line; do
  if echo "$line" | grep -q "initialize"; then
    echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"tools":true}}}'
  fi
done

-- replay_trace.mcp --
# mcptrace:v1
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1} # 1000.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}} # 1001.000

# Test mcpcat
exec mcpcat test1.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv'
stdout 'mcp-send'
stdout 'initialize'

# Test mcpcat with color disabled
exec mcpcat -color=never test1.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv'
stdout 'mcp-send'
! stdout '\033'  # No ANSI color codes

# Test mcp-sort with strip
exec mcp-sort -strip test1.mcp
stdout '\[TIMESTAMP\]'

# Test mcp-sort without strip
exec mcp-sort test1.mcp
stdout '\[2024-01-01'

# Test mcpdiff
cp test2.mcp test3.mcp
echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"new","id":3} # 1004.000' >> test3.mcp

# Should return exit code 1 for differences
! exec mcpdiff test2.mcp test3.mcp
stdout 'new'

# Should return exit code 0 for identical files
exec mcpdiff test2.mcp test2.mcp

# Test mcpdiff compare mode with shadow trace
exec mcpdiff -compare shadow.mcp
stdout 'tools\|capabilities'

# Test mcpcat with shadow traces
exec mcpcat shadow.mcp
stdout 'mcp-send-shadow'

# Test mcpspy with a simple command
exec mcpspy -f spy.mcp cat input.txt
stdout 'test data'
exists spy.mcp

# Verify mcpspy recorded something
exec cat spy.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv\|mcp-send'

# Test mcp-shadow (if available)
chmod +x primary_server.sh
chmod +x shadow_server.sh

# Note: mcp-shadow test commented out as it requires specific setup
# setstdin '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1}'
# exec mcp-shadow --primary "./primary_server.sh" --shadow "./shadow_server.sh" --compare -o shadow_output.mcp

# Test mcp-replay in mock server mode (non-blocking test)
# exec mcp-replay -mock-server -f replay_trace.mcp -o replay_output.mcp &

# Test sorting with out-of-order timestamps
exec mcp-sort unsorted.mcp
stdout -count=3 'mcp-'

# Test pipeline: mcpspy -> mcpcat -> mcp-sort
setstdin '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test","id":1}'
exec cat | exec mcpspy -f pipeline.mcp cat > /dev/null
exists pipeline.mcp

exec mcpcat -color=never pipeline.mcp 
stdout 'mcp-recv'

exec mcp-sort -strip pipeline.mcp
stdout '\[TIMESTAMP\]'
stdout 'mcp-recv'

# Check all expected files are created
exists test1.mcp
exists test2.mcp
exists test3.mcp
exists shadow.mcp
exists spy.mcp
exists unsorted.mcp
exists replay_trace.mcp
exists pipeline.mcp

# Clean up
rm test3.mcp spy.mcp pipeline.mcp
